topics.title: Individual Differences in Brain Connectivity and States
topics.description: This cluster centers on how fMRI-derived brain connectivity and dynamic brain states vary across individuals, with emphasis on reliability, parcellation, and fingerprinting. It also highlights emerging foundation-model and prediction approaches for understanding human connectome organization and brain dynamics.

topics.title: Neurodegenerative Disease Progression and Biomarkers
topics.description: This cluster focuses on clinical and biomarker patterns across major neurodegenerative disorders, especially Parkinson and Alzheimer disease, dementia, and related cognitive decline. It also highlights prodromal and preclinical states, white matter changes, and Lewy body-associated symptoms.

topics.title: Neural Bases of Emotion Regulation and Affective Processing
topics.description: This cluster centers on neural correlates of emotion regulation, affective processing, and related social-emotional phenomena such as anxiety, fear, empathy, and stress. It also includes work on brain mechanisms and representations underlying these processes, often involving the amygdala and fMRI.

topics.title: Depression Treatment and Brain Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on depressive disorders, especially treatment-resistant depression, and links clinical symptoms and treatment response to brain network alterations. It highlights neuromodulation and imaging findings involving functional connectivity, the default mode network, DLPFC, and ACC.

topics.title: Deep Learning for Brain MRI Segmentation
topics.description: This cluster centers on machine learning and deep learning methods for MRI-based brain mapping, segmentation, and reconstruction. It includes atlas building, cortical folding analysis, and comparisons with tools such as FreeSurfer and SynthSeg.

topics.title: Brain Development Across Childhood and Adolescence
topics.description: This cluster centers on neurodevelopmental change from infancy through adolescence, including normative and altered developmental trajectories. It also highlights the effects of prematurity and early-life factors on brain development and later cognitive outcomes.

topics.title: White Matter Microstructure Across the Lifespan
topics.description: This cluster focuses on white matter microstructure and diffusion MRI measures across development, adolescence, adulthood, and lifespan change. It includes work on multiple sclerosis and normative modelling to characterize white matter tract and superficial white matter alterations.

topics.title: Genetic and Structural Risk in Psychosis
topics.description: This cluster centers on schizophrenia and related psychosis spectrum disorders, emphasizing genetic risk, early life adversity, and familial high-risk populations. It also highlights brain structure, biomarkers, and transdiagnostic heterogeneity across neuropsychiatric illness.

topics.title: Visual Representation and Decoding in the Human Brain
topics.description: This cluster centers on how visual information and imagery are encoded in the human visual cortex and related brain regions. It also includes work using fMRI decoding and deep neural networks to study neural representation and functional organization.

topics.title: Brain Aging and Cognitive Decline
topics.description: Studies in this cluster examine aging-related changes in the brain across the adult lifespan, including brain age, brain structure, and cognitive outcomes. Many abstracts focus on links between aging, cognitive reserve, and dementia risk in healthy aging and later life.

topics.title: Stroke-related motor and language recovery
topics.description: This cluster centers on stroke and focal brain lesions, with emphasis on how structural disconnection, motor cortex involvement, and lesion location relate to functional recovery, motor outcome, and aphasia. It includes studies of motor control, movement, and cognitive deficits after acute and ischemic stroke.

topics.title: Epilepsy Network Localization and Response
topics.description: This cluster centers on epilepsy subtypes, seizure localization, and network alterations underlying disease expression. It also includes studies of invasive and scalp electrophysiology used to characterize epileptogenic zones and predict therapy response.

topics.title: High-Resolution fMRI Methods in Small Animals
topics.description: The cluster centers on advanced fMRI acquisition and analysis approaches for small-animal imaging, especially at ultra-high field strengths. Emphasis is placed on improving BOLD sensitivity, reducing artifacts and head motion effects, and optimizing protocols for functional and vascular mapping.

topics.title: Neural Bases of Language and Speech
topics.description: This cluster centers on how language and speech are represented and processed in the brain, including auditory cortex involvement, language network organization, and neural correlates of comprehension. It also includes studies of multilingual speakers, aphasia, lateralization, and age-related differences in language function.

topics.title: Open Neuroimaging Pipelines and Standards
topics.description: This cluster centers on open, standardized neuroimaging workflows, including data conversion, processing pipelines, and tools for scalable analysis. It also reflects interest in reproducible infrastructure, visualization, and harmonization across datasets and studies.

keywords: eeg resting state complexity and entropy
topics.title: Sleep States and Brain Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on sleep, consciousness, and the dynamic brain activity underlying transitions between behavioral states. It also includes aging-related sleep patterns and EEG-based measures of complexity and connectivity during sleep.

topics.title: EEG Dynamics in Attention and Interoception
topics.description: This cluster centers on EEG-based investigation of how alpha and aperiodic neural activity relate to attention, interoception, and brain-body coupling. It also includes studies of modulation and dynamic changes in cortical activity across cognitive states.

keywords: autism sensory subtypes predict task evoked insula connectivity
topics.title: Autism Subtypes and Brain Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on autism and autism spectrum disorder, with emphasis on subgroup differences, sensory subtypes, and developmental variation across autistic children, youth, and adults. The abstracts also highlight brain abnormalities and connectivity findings, especially fMRI-based markers linked to clinically distinct autism presentations.

keywords: coil orientation modulate tms induce changes in
topics.title: Neuromodulation, Connectivity, and Brain Circuit Targeting
topics.description: This cluster centers on noninvasive and invasive brain stimulation approaches, especially TMS, iTBS, and deep brain stimulation, across disorders such as Parkinson disease. It also highlights circuit-level targeting, connectivity-based prediction, and stimulation parameters such as coil orientation and individualized neuromodulation.

keywords: functional connectivity pathways support fear avoidance processes
topics.title: Neural Bases of Pain and Sensitization
topics.description: This cluster centers on chronic and experimental pain conditions, including migraine, trigeminal neuralgia, and osteoarthritis, with emphasis on pain perception, analgesia, and hyperalgesia. It also highlights neural mechanisms such as central sensitization, cortical pain phenotypes, and fear-avoidance related connectivity pathways.

keywords: functional connectivity predict crave across substance use disorder
functional neural predictors of early alcohol initiation in emerging adolescence
topics.title: Neural Predictors of Substance Use and Craving
topics.description: This cluster centers on addiction-related disorders, craving, reward, and abstinence, with emphasis on neural circuitry and functional imaging markers across substances. It also includes studies of predictors of early alcohol initiation and craving-related functional connectivity.

keywords: functional and metabolic network signatures of
topics.title: Human Brain Neurometabolic Network Signatures
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how glucose metabolism, bioenergetic challenges, and glymphatic processes shape functional and metabolic organization in the human brain. It emphasizes connectomics, network signatures, and multimodal MRS-based mapping of neurometabolic patterns across brain regions.

topics.title: UK Biobank fMRI Biomarker Studies
topics.description: Research centered on the UK Biobank study and related UK Biobank analyses using fMRI biomarker approaches. The cluster appears to focus on measurement-oriented neuroimaging work rather than a specific biological theme.
